explanation: The requirement can be abstracted as: Whenever the ventilator is in PCV or PSV mode, there must be the possibility to press a single button (inspiratoryPauseButton) to initiate an inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPause), which closes both inspiratory (inValveClose) and expiratory valves (outValveClose) at the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), for as long as the operator holds the button but no longer than 40 seconds. In LTL, this is typically modeled as: Whenever (PCVMode OR PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd holds, then (if inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, then for up to 40 seconds, inspiratoryPause, inValveClose, and outValveClose hold, stopping when the button is released or the time limit is reached). However, LTL does not have native time-bounded operators, so the 'no longer than 40 seconds' constraint (if mapping 1 step = 1 sec) is modeled using the 'for up to 40 steps' idiom. Since not all atomic propositions are provided for mode switching and time handling, we'll formulate the core safety property as: Whenever (PCVMode OR PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd and inspiratoryPauseButton holds, then (inspiratoryPause AND inValveClose AND outValveClose) holds until (!inspiratoryPauseButton OR 40 seconds passed). In LTL, time-bounded constraints are usually represented with auxiliary variables/counters or using U (until) with a big disjunction of 'within N steps'. For simplicity, we'll capture the essence: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton))).

explanation_dict.natural_language: Ventilator is in PCV or PSV mode
At the end of the inspiratory phase
Initiate an inspiratory pause that closes both inspiratory and expiratory valves while the button is held
Globally, whenever mode, phase end, and button hold, the pause and valve conditions hold until button released
explanation_dict.LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)))

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) U (!inspiratoryPauseButton)))

explanation:
First, we identify the key requirements in the natural language:
- In PCV and PSV mode (PCVMode or PSVMode). 
- There shall be the possibility to press a single button (existence of inspiratoryPauseButton).
- Initiate an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause) whenever the button is pressed.
- This closes both inspiratory and expiratory valve (i.e., inValveClose & outValveClose).
- At the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), the pause can be activated.
- The pause continues as long as the button is held, but at most for 40 seconds (can be translated as: after at most 40 steps, the condition must stop holding, assuming a time step is 1 second).

Translating into LTL:
1. 'Whenever in PCV or PSV mode': G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> ... )
2. 'There shall be the possibility... to initiate an Inspiratory Pause by pressing a button': for every time in PCV or PSV, F(inspiratoryPauseButton), but a stricter interpretation is: if button pressed at the correct time, the pause starts.
3. 'Inspiratory Pause starts when button pressed at inspiratoryPhaseEnd': whenever inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPhaseEnd, then initiates inspiratoryPause for as long as the button is held, but no longer than 40 sec.
4. 'As long as the operator holds the button': inspiratoryPause should hold while inspiratoryPauseButton holds, but (no longer than 40 sec).
5. 'Both valves closed while inspiratoryPause': inValveClose & outValveClose must be true when inspiratoryPause holds.

So:
A. Whenever in PCV or PSV, whenever inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPhaseEnd: Start inspiratoryPause.
B. Once inspiratoryPause starts, inspiratoryPause continues for as long as inspiratoryPauseButton holds, but no longer than 40 seconds. When inspiratoryPause, inValveClose and outValveClose.

Given LTL's limitations, and atomic props, we write:
G(
  ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton)
    -> (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose & (inspiratoryPause U ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) | F_<=40 (!inspiratoryPause))))
)
Where F_<=40 is an abbreviation (not LTL-standard). In LTL, a common over-approximation is a bound on the length, but not expressible in pure LTL unless time is encoded.

Pragmatically, the core LTL specification is:
G(
  ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) ->
    (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose &
     (inspiratoryPause U ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) | X^40 !inspiratoryPause)))
)
Thus: Whenever (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd and button pressed, then inspiratoryPause, both valves close, and inspiratoryPause holds until the button is released or 40 steps have passed.

For pure LTL with the allowed operators, we cannot write X^40, so the time-bounded part is left implicit or would be handled via auxiliary variables.

Therefore, the main property:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose & (inspiratoryPause U !inspiratoryPauseButton)))

This covers: InspiratoryPause starts if button is pressed at end of inspiration in PCV/PSV, and holds as long as button is held (optionally, up to 40s in implementation).

explanation_dict.natural_language: System is in PCV or PSV mode
Button is pressed at the end of inspiratory phase
If (PCVMode or PSVMode) and inspiratoryPhaseEnd and inspiratoryPauseButton, then inspiratoryPause starts and both valves close
InspiratoryPause holds as long as button is held (pause holds until button released)

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose & (inspiratoryPause U !inspiratoryPauseButton)))

explanation: Given the list of allowed atomic propositions, we cannot express time bounds or arbitrary propositions like 'a', 'b', or 'c' unless they are mapped to valid atomic propositions. For the medical example, we omit the 40s time bound as before and encode: If we are in PCVMode or PSVMode, and at the end of the inspiratory phase, with user_equalTo_operator and inspiratoryPauseButton pressed, then (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) holds until the button is released.

explanation_dict.natural_language: At end of inspiratory phase and operator presses Inspiratory Pause button
Inspiratory Pause is active, both valves closed as long as button is held

explanation_dict.LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & user_equalTo_operator & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> ((inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) U (¬inspiratoryPauseButton)))

explanation: To express 'Globally if a holds then c is true until b' with only the given atomic propositions, "a", "b", and "c" must be mapped to some valid atomic propositions. Since a, b, and c are not in the allowed list, we cannot produce a valid formula unless you specify to which atomic propositions they should correspond. As an example, if we map a to 'PSVMode', c to 'inspiratoryPause', and b to 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd', the LTL translation would be: G(PSVMode -> (inspiratoryPause U inspiratoryPhaseEnd)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: 'c is true until b' mapped to inspiratoryPause U inspiratoryPhaseEnd
If a then c U b: PSVMode -> inspiratoryPause U inspiratoryPhaseEnd

explanation: Atomic propositions 'r' and 'g' are not in the allowed set. To translate 'Every request r is eventually followed by a grant g' with propositions from the allowed set, you must specify which atomic propositions correspond to r and g. As an example, if r is 'patientBreathTrigger' and g is 'inspiratoryPause', then the translation would be G(patientBreathTrigger -> F inspiratoryPause).

We are to formalize: In PCV or PSV ventilation mode, there must be the possibility to press a single button (inspiratoryPauseButton) at the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd) to initiate an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause), where both valves (inValveClose and outValveClose) are closed for as long as the button is held and no longer than 40 seconds. LTL cannot directly encode finite time bounds like 'no longer than 40 seconds' unless the system is suitably discretized (e.g., one step = one second), but we can specify the core logical dependencies, and that pressing the button at phase end causes the pause/valve states as long as the button is held. We'll note the time bound in the explanation.

explanation_dict.natural_language: PCV or PSV ventilation mode is active
At the end of inspiratory phase and pause button is pressed
If in PCV/PSV mode, and the pause button is pressed at inspiratory phase end, then immediately an inspiratory pause is engaged and both valves are closed.
explanation_dict.LTL: ((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> X (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose)

explanation_dict.natural_language: While the pause button is held, inspiratory pause and both valves remain active (and at least never more than 40 steps, where each step represents a time unit; time bound is noted in the explanation, not formula).
If the pause button is released, the inspiratory pause ends (pause and valve closures are released).

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> X (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose)) & G(inspiratoryPause -> (inspiratoryPauseButton & inValveClose & outValveClose)) & G((inspiratoryPause & !inspiratoryPauseButton) -> X (!inspiratoryPause & !inValveClose & !outValveClose))

explanation:
Given your constraint on atomic propositions, we cannot explicitly encode real-time constraints, so we only use your provided atomic propositions. 
1. In PCV or PSV mode, at the end of the inspiratory phase, if the operator holds the inspiratoryPauseButton, then inspiratoryPause becomes active (as long as the button stays pressed).
2. While inspiratoryPause is active, both inValveClose and outValveClose are true. The inspiratoryPause ends as soon as the button is released. 
The formula is thus:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F(inspiratoryPause))
G(inspiratoryPause -> (inValveClose & outValveClose & inspiratoryPauseButton))
This expresses the two key obligations: the pause is entered after the button is pressed in the correct context, and both valves are closed as long as the pause and the button press last.

explanation_dict.natural_language: System is in PCV or PSV mode
As long as inspiratoryPause is active, inspiratoryPauseButton is also true, so both valves are closed
Whenever (PCV/PSV mode, inspiratoryPhaseEnd and button pressed), pause is eventually entered

output_LTL: G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> F(inspiratoryPause)) & G(inspiratoryPause -> (inValveClose & outValveClose & inspiratoryPauseButton))

explanation: The translation uses only valid atomic propositions. Suppose atomic proposition a maps to 'logO2SensorUse', c to 'inValveOpen', and b to 'testPowerSwitchPass'. The structure is: Globally, if logO2SensorUse holds, then inValveOpen is true until testPowerSwitchPass. So, the LTL is: G(logO2SensorUse -> (inValveOpen U testPowerSwitchPass)).

explanation_dict.natural_language: if a holds then c is true until b

explanation: Using only valid atomic propositions: let r be 'patientBreathTrigger' (request) and g be 'ventilating' (grant). The LTL formula: G(patientBreathTrigger -> F ventilating) states that every patient breath trigger is eventually followed by ventilation.

We interpret this requirement as: In PCV or PSV mode, there must exist a way to press a button (inspiratoryPauseButton) to trigger an Inspiratory Pause (inspiratoryPause), which ensures inValveClose (inspiratory valve closed) and outValveClose (expiratory valve closed), and the pause lasts as long as the operator holds the button but not longer than 40 seconds, after which normal operation resumes.

Step-by-step:
1. "In PCV and PSV mode": (PCVMode | PSVMode) — the system is in either mode.
2. "possibility to press a single button to initiate an Inspiratory Pause": The atomic proposition inspiratoryPauseButton represents this.
3. "initiate an Inspiratory Pause (IP)": inspiratoryPause indicates when the pause is active.
4. "that closes both inspiratory and expiratory valve": inValveClose & outValveClose are true.
5. "at the end of the inspiratory phase": The pause can only be initiated at inspiratoryPhaseEnd (or immediately after).
6. "as long as operator holds the button but no longer than 40 sec": This is most precisely modeled in LTL as: As soon as inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed at the end of the inspiratory phase, inspiratoryPause holds and so do inValveClose and outValveClose, for as long as inspiratoryPauseButton is held and, at most, for 40 steps (assuming time discretized as steps). After the operator releases the button or 40 seconds have passed, the pause ends.

This can be constructed as: 
Globally, in each inspiratoryPhaseEnd, if (PCVMode | PSVMode) and inspiratoryPauseButton is pressed, then inspiratoryPause, inValveClose, and outValveClose will be true at the next step and stay true as long as inspiratoryPauseButton stays pressed, for at most 40 steps.
If the button is released or 40 steps have passed, the pause is deactivated.

A possible LTL formalization for the main guarantee is (where []=G, ()=parentheses):

G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & X(inspiratoryPhaseEnd) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> 
    (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose & 
         ((inspiratoryPauseButton & t<40) U ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) | (t=40)))
    )
)
However, LTL has no natural way to count steps (no direct t<40), so the 'no longer than 40 sec' must be left as a comment or handled with an auxiliary counter. Without the counter, we express:

G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & X(inspiratoryPhaseEnd) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> 
    ((inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) U ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) | timeout40sec))
)
Where timeout40sec is not an atomic proposition, but for pure LTL we generally express only the button holding part.

Final LTL (limited by LTL expressiveness, without explicit time):
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> (inspiratoryPauseButton -> (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose)))

But to capture releasing the button ends the pause, it can be formulated as:
G((PCVMode | PSVMode) -> ((inspiratoryPauseButton U ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) | timeout40sec)) -> (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose) U ((!inspiratoryPauseButton) | timeout40sec)))

For practical purposes, with pure LTL and available atomic props, the main guarantee is:
G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton) -> (inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose))
Globally, whenever the system is in PCV or PSV mode and the Inspiratory Pause button is pressed, the Inspiratory Pause is active and both valves are closed.

explanation_dict.natural_language: So long as the button is held

explanation_dict.LTL: Cannot be directly represented in pure LTL; needs auxiliary variable.
